Bottom line: Upgrades are an experience investment. Pair them with the right staffing so everything feels smooth, special, and safe.

title: Band & Live Music Setup
description: Stage availability, band parking, and equipment storage for live music at Rixey.
content:
Band & Live Music Setup

We do not have a stage on-site. The patio works perfectly for most bands — it's included in the table layout and many band contracts already account for a patio-level setup. If your band specifically requires an elevated stage (e.g., 24x16), you can rent one through Sammy's Rentals.

Band parking and load-in: Bands can unload around back straight into the tent/patio area, then repark their vehicle (van, trailer, etc.) in the designated parking area. Trailers up to 33' in length can be accommodated.

Equipment storage: Empty instrument cases can stay on-site during the event — no need to load them back on the trailer until strike.

The patio space is allocated in the standard table layouts, so your planner and the band will both know where the performance area is.

title: Cake Drop-Off & Storage
description: When and how wedding cakes can be delivered and stored at the venue.
content:
Cake Drop-Off & Storage

Cake can be dropped off on Friday (the day before a Saturday wedding). The baker should coordinate timing with the venue — schedule via Calendly or text (540) 212-4545.

We have fridge space available for cakes that need refrigeration. Just let us know in advance so we can make room.

The cake is typically displayed on a 30" hightop behind the sweetheart table or estate table. If you're using a separate dessert table or sweets display (e.g., Sweets by Amanda), we can set up additional tables in the cake area.

For donut walls, cookie tables, or other dessert displays: these can also be set up Friday or Saturday morning depending on the item. Anything perishable should come Saturday; shelf-stable items can come Friday.

title: Fire Pit
content:
Fire Pit

The fire pit is available for use at your wedding — just let us know you'd like it. It's great for:
- Chilly spring or fall evenings when guests want to gather outside
- Creating a cozy atmosphere after sunset
- S'mores stations or late-night hangout vibes

We'll have it ready to go for your event. No special arrangements needed beyond letting us know in advance.

title: Rain Plan & Weather Backup
description: What happens if it rains — ceremony backup, tent options, and how weather decisions are made.
content:
Rain Plan & Weather Backup

Ceremony: If rain is expected, the ceremony moves indoors to the ballroom (seats up to ~100 for a ceremony setup). For larger guest counts, the tent with sides down is the backup. We monitor the weather closely in the days leading up and will proactively reach out if the forecast looks concerning.

Tent: The tent sides can be lowered for wind or light rain. For heavy rain, the tent provides full coverage. We set the arbor up on Saturday morning (even if a rental company delivers it Friday, we'll store it inside Friday night just in case).

How the decision gets made: We don't make the call too early. Weather in Rixeyville can change quickly. We'll discuss options with you the week of and make the final call together, usually the morning of. We always have a Plan B ready so the pivot is seamless if needed.

For hot weather specifically: see the Tent Cooling & AC entry.

title: Tent Cooling & AC
description: How cooling works across the venue buildings and tent, especially for summer weddings.
content:
Tent Cooling & AC

The main house has full AC throughout. The ballroom has commercial AC. The bride's suite can get warm when multiple hair dryers are running simultaneously.

For the tent: portable AC units don't reliably work in our experience (we've only tested them on 100°+ days). Fans may not help much either — they can make it hard to take the tent sides down.

What does work:
- White tent (recommended for June, July, August): creates shade coverage instead of amplifying the sun like a clear tent does.
- Strategic side peeling: we open specific sides to create a cross breeze.
- We always have sunglasses and hand fans on hand and will offer them to guests who look like they could use them.
- Once the sun goes down, the temperature drops quickly and it becomes very pleasant for dancing and the rest of the evening.

If couples are concerned about a hot forecast, they can visit the tent during the hottest part of the day before their wedding to see how it feels. For example, if the tent is up Wednesday and it's going to be 83°, stand in there at the hottest point and judge for yourself.

Bottom line: there's no magic solution for a hot tent afternoon, but the discomfort is usually limited to about 30 minutes before the sun shifts.

title: Tent Delivery & Setup
description: How tent delivery and setup works — couples don't need to coordinate with Sammy's directly.
content:
Tent Delivery & Setup

Sammy's handles their own delivery schedule — the venue coordinates directly with them. You do NOT need to call Sammy's to specify delivery times.

Typical timeline: Tent is set up Thursday or Friday before a Saturday wedding. Tent sides can be raised or lowered depending on weather and airflow needs.

For tent modifications on the wedding day: we can strategically remove sides for cross breeze on hot days, or lower sides for wind/rain protection. These decisions are made day-of based on conditions.

If you've ordered heaters through Sammy's, monitor the forecast the week of — if temps are warm, consider canceling heaters to avoid being charged for something you don't need.

title: Ballroom Capacity
description: Maximum seated capacity for the ballroom and how it affects layout decisions.
content:
Ballroom Capacity

The ballroom holds up to approximately 100 guests for a seated dinner.

For 120-130+ guests: The reception is typically held under the tent (standard 40x70 from Sammy's). The ballroom becomes the cocktail hour space, bar area, and later the dance floor. This gives you the best of both worlds — romantic tent dining and an intimate ballroom party atmosphere near the bar.

For 100-110 guests: A full indoor reception in the ballroom is possible but requires careful planning for dining, dancing, and cocktail areas. It can feel tight. Consider whether you want dedicated cocktail hour space separate from the dining space.

Many couples use a transitional flow: first dances on the patio under the tent, then transition to the ballroom for party dancing (while caterers clear tent stations during the transition — they need about 15-20 minutes).

title: Chair Inventory
description: How many chairs Rixey has on-site and options for supplementing.
content:
Chair Inventory

Rixey Manor currently has approximately 170 white Chiavari chairs (and we're buying more). These are included in your venue rental and are used for both ceremony and reception.

If your guest count exceeds our chair inventory, you can supplement with additional chairs through your tent rental company (typically Sammy's Rentals). Cross-back chairs are a popular option through Sammy's — estimate your total ceremony + reception seating needs and order accordingly. You can adjust quantities after RSVPs come in.

Ceremony chairs are pre-set by Rixey staff before vendor arrival. After the ceremony, chairs are moved to the reception area by the staffing team (caterer crew + Rixey extra hands). Weather may slightly affect whether all chairs get moved or some sets get put away.

title: Table Layout Options
description: Examples of table shapes, capacities, and placement ideas.
content: Table Layout Options Highlights Rixey offers multiple table sizes: rounds, rectangles, sweetheart, and cocktail tables. Max guest count: ~150 using all connected spaces. Round tables: 72” seats 10 comfortably; 60” seats 8. Rectangular tables: 6’ seats 6; 8’ seats 8. Combine for estate or head tables. Sweetheart table (for 2) and cocktail rounds (6 available) included. Full inventory : 11 x 6’ rounds, 10 x 5’ rounds, 9 x 6’ rectangles, 8 x 8’ rectangles, 2 x 4’ sweetheart tables, 6 cocktail rounds. Linens: specific sizes recommended for each table. Don’t forget extra tables for gift, guest book, photo booth, desserts, buffet, kids’ table, DJ, etc. Photo Booth/Band/Large Decor/Stations is not accounted for in those numbers so please let us know before you commit to something requiring a larger than 10ft footprint outside of the normal tables and chairs. Layout options tailored at Final Walkthrough. Comfort prioritized over maxing capacity. Table Sizes & Capacities 72” Round (6 ft round): Seats 10 comfortably (12 max if not using chargers). With chargers, max is 10 for comfort. 60” Round (5 ft round): Seats 8 comfortably (10 max if cozy and no chargers). We recommend 8. 6’ x 30” Rectangular: Seats 6 (3 per side). Add 1 on each end (total 8) if no chargers, but 6 is best. 8’ x 30” Rectangular: Seats 8 (4 per side). With ends, can fit 10, but 8 is most comfortable. Two Rectangles Joined: Two 6’ tables end-to-end = 12’ table = 12 seats (6 per side). Two 8’ tables end-to-end = 16’ table = 16 seats (8 per side). If you are only seating guests on one side - for a head table - half that number. Ends typically left empty to avoid seam seating. Sweetheart Table: Special 4’ x 30” table for 2 people (we have two available). Cocktail Tables: 30” round (6 available). Can be tall (42”) or standard (30”). Usually no chairs. Inventory Available 11 x 72” rounds (6 ft) 10 x 60” rounds (5 ft) 9 x 6’ rectangles 8 x 8’ rectangles 2 x 4’ sweetheart tables 6 cocktail tables Enough for all configurations up to ~150 guests. Final numbers set at Final Walkthrough. Linen Sizes Cocktail (30” round): 132” round linen (floor-length; tie with sash for elegance). Cake Table (30” round): 132” round (puddles slightly, that’s fine). 4’ Rectangular (sweetheart): 88” x 132” rectangular. 5’ Round (60”): 120” round linen. 6’ Round (72”): 132” round linen. 6’ Rectangular: 88” x 132” rectangular. 8’ Rectangular: 88” x 154” (two 8’ tables = ~88” x 190”). ⚠️ Don’t forget linens for extra tables (gift, guest book, beverage, etc.). Most commonly overlooked: two 88” x 132” linens for guest book/cards table and beverages table . Specialty & Additional Tables Gift & Card Table: 6’ table on porch or patio, later moved inside for safety. Guest Book Table: Sometimes combined with gift table; later relocated inside. Pre-Ceremony Drink Table: 6’ table for lemonade/cider, usually on porch/patio. Seating Chart: Escort cards → 6’ or round table. Large chart → easel. Photo Booth Prop Table: Small table in bar area. Dessert Table: 6’ or round for cupcakes/cookies/candy buffet. S’mores Table: 6’ table on patio, dark linen recommended (messy!). Cocktail Rounds: Up to 6 placed around dance floor/patio. Buffet Tables: Up to 3 x 6’ tables end-to-end (18’ buffet). Needs 3 x 132” linens. Kids’ Activity Table: Round or rectangle in a corner; butcher paper option available. DJ Table: 6’ rectangle. Near outlets. Sweetheart/Head Table: Sweetheart = 4’ table for 2. Head = series of rectangles (e.g., three 8’ tables for 18 people). Long “estate” tables possible but not recommended for >100 guests. Layout Tips Ballroom: Fits ~10 round tables of 6’ = ~100 guests. Patio: Used for overflow under a tent if >100 guests. Front Lawn: Great for outdoor dining under stars/tents. Dance Floor Placement: Usually center ballroom. Sometimes expands post-dinner by moving tables. High Chairs: Two available; placed as requested. Multiple Rooms: Dining room, library, porch can be used for overflow. We’ll pipe in sound for speeches. Finalizing Layout Options/templates presented at Final Walkthrough (4–6 weeks out). Every layout customized to your guest count and style. Comfort prioritized: we avoid cramming max capacity. Happy guests > tight squeeze. Reception tables usually set Friday; ceremony chairs set day-of (weather). Couples/families often place favors/decor Friday after tables are positioned. Summary We’ll help you create a reception layout that balances space, style, and guest comfort. From cozy dinners of 40 to lively parties of 150, Rixey Manor’s rooms and inventory offer flexibility. Below are 10 starting point layouts from 50 to 200 people. Auxiliary tables are placed on a case by case basis and discussed at final walkthrough.

title: Google Planning Sheet
description: Details and guidelines for filling in your planning sheet.
content: Google Planning Sheet We provide a collaborative Google Sheets workbook customized for your wedding. This planning sheet has multiple tabs (pages) covering everything from your timeline, vendor contacts, and layout plans to décor checklists and music selections. It’s a living document that both you and our team can update, ensuring everyone stays on the same page. A Few Important Notes Before You Begin: If you cannot open or access this sheet — email me immediately so I can resend it or adjust permissions. Don’t wait. Only fill in the tabs that apply to your wedding. If a tab doesn’t apply (e.g. no rehearsal dinner, no shuttle, no bedroom assignments), feel free to delete it or simply leave it blank. You may write comments anywhere — questions, “not sure yet,” or options you’re debating. I’d rather see a half-filled plan than wait for perfection. If you leave notes like “Need Instructions” or “Not sure what to do here,” I’ll guide you during our next check-in. If you’d like instructions on how to edit in Google Sheets , how to highlight cells , or how to add comments , I’m happy to add a Technology Help tab — just say the word. Top Sheet — Start Here (But Don’t Stress About Completing It Yet) This is your quick-glance overview of the weekend — the sheet we refer back to constantly when coordinating with vendors, checking timing, or solving last-minute mysteries (“What time is the rehearsal again?”). You do not need to fill in every box right away. Just start with what you know. We will go back through every field together during your Final Walkthrough. Here’s how to approach it: Names & Parents’ Names — Fill in yours and your parents’ full names. This helps with welcome speeches, seating, and staff knowing who’s who. “Have We Met Them?” — Just write Yes / No / Met Once / Not Yet — this lets us know whether intros are needed. Rehearsal Time and Rehearsal Guest Count — Fill it in if you’ve already decided. If not sure yet, leave it. Rehearsal Dinner Onsite or Offsite? — Just write Onsite or Offsite. Details come later. Wedding Colors — Loose description is fine (e.g. Dusty Blue + Sage, or Autumn Florals). Helps staff match linens and decor placement. Wedding Date / Guest Count / Ceremony Start Time / Sunset Please fill these in early — these drive all scheduling (ceremony lighting, photography, shuttle timing, etc.). Allergies Section Leave blank if there are none yet — otherwise list: Who the allergy is for What it is (e.g. nuts, shellfish, bees) Severity (e.g. mild / EpiPen / anaphylactic) Have you alerted the caterer? — Yes/No Staying overnight? — Only matters for Manor guests Extra Staff Section You’ll see a reminder of Rixey’s staffing policy here — 1 bartender per X guests, and Extra Hands for setup/cleanup if full-service catering isn’t handling it. You don’t need to know final numbers yet. For now, just answer roughly: How many bartenders you expect to need each day (Fri/Sat/Sun) How many Extra Hands you expect — if you’re unsure, write “Not sure yet” and we’ll decide together. Venmo Handle — please fill this in early so we can coordinate payment smoothly. Remember: This tab is meant to start the conversation, not finish it.Fill in what you can — even half answers are fine — and we’ll review every row line by line together at the Final Walkthrough. Questions — Please Answer When You Can This tab is simply a list of questions we’ll need answered before the wedding. If you already know the answer — fill it in.If you don’t — leave it blank and we’ll go over it together at your Final Walkthrough. No pressure to complete everything at once. This is just where we collect decisions so they’re all in one place. Vendors Please fill out one row per vendor. If you don’t have all the details yet, just fill in what you know — blanks are fine and we’ll finish it together at your Final Walkthrough. Column Header What to Enter Vendor The type of service they are providing (e.g. Catering for Wedding, DJ, Florist, Shuttle Service). Keep categories consistent so it’s easy to skim. Name The business name or individual’s name (whichever is most accurate to who is actually showing up). Contact Phone Number Best direct number for day-of coordination. If they have both office and cell, use who will be on-site. Arrival Time When they are scheduled to arrive for setup. If unsure, leave blank and we’ll confirm later. Departure Time When they expect to leave / finish breakdown. Leave blank if not known yet. Have they worked here before? Yes / No / Not Sure — this just helps us know how much direction they’ll need on-site. Have you uploaded contract to HB? Yes / No — this is to confirm whether their contract is already stored in HoneyBook. Instagram Handle (if you know it) Optional — add if you'd like us to tag vendors in social media posts afterward. Notes: Any special instructions, delivery timing requests, or reminders (e.g. “Deliver Thursday,” “Needs access to power,” “Bringing their own ice”). Friday & Sunday Timeline — Fill As You Know It, We’ll Finalize Together This tab helps organize who’s arriving when and what’s happening on Friday (check-in / rehearsal) and Sunday (checkout / brunch or cleanup).You do not need to fill in everything at once — just start entering times and vendors as plans come together. We will confirm all timing and logistics at your Final Walkthrough. Friday Section (Check-In / Rehearsal Day) There are two parts to fill: Vendors Involved (Top Table) List only the vendors who are delivering or setting up on Friday. Fill in Vendor Type / Company Name / Contact / Arrival / Departure if known. If you’re unsure of timing yet — leave it blank. Timeline (Time Block Table) Use the Time Block / Time / Event / Those Involved / Notes fields to map out the flow. Start with what you know (e.g. “4:00 PM Check-In”, “5:30 PM Rehearsal”). Leave anything blank that is still TBD — we will line it up with you later. At the bottom is a Rehearsal Dinner Question Section — simply answer each question in the blue cells next to it. Short answers are fine. Sunday Section (Checkout / Brunch / Cleanup) Same format as Friday — Vendor Table on top, Timeline Table below. Only fill this section if you’re staying for Sunday brunch or hosting guests onsite. If you’re not doing a brunch or extended stay — just write “Not Staying Sunday” at the top of the Sunday section. You’ll also see an “Average Time Estimates” reminder (e.g. Cleaning Up = 2 hours). These are just guidelines — feel free to adjust based on your plans. Reminder: Start light — even half-filled is perfect.Anything unconfirmed will be locked in during your Final Walkthrough Saturday Timeline — Start with Just the Two Key Details This tab doesn’t need to be perfect — it just gives us your starting outline for how you picture the day flowing. Before anything else, please answer: What time does your ceremony begin? Will you be doing a First Look? (Yes / No / Not Sure Yet is fine.) Once those are filled in: If you already have a general order in mind (e.g. Photos → Ceremony → Cocktail Hour → Dinner → Dancing), start adding things into the Time / Event / Those Involved columns. If not — leave it blank. We’ll build the full schedule with you using the sample timelines in the dedicated Timeline Example section later in the sheet. You can also write notes like “Want sunset photos before dinner” or “Prefer longer cocktail hour” — even loose preferences are helpful. No need for exact minutes — just the anchors.Ceremony time + First Look answer are the only must-haves right now. Shuttle Schedule — Fill This Once You Know Ceremony Time Use this tab to lay out all shuttle runs to and from the hotel/venue. You’ll see two sets of tables: A blank version at the top (for you to fill out) A sample version underneath (for reference only — do not edit that one) How to fill the blank table: Column What to Enter Shuttle 1 / Shuttle 2 Each column is one vehicle. If you only have one shuttle, just use the first column. Pick up at Hotel Enter the pickup time from the hotel/lodging. Drop off at Manor Enter the expected arrival time at Rixey. …then alternate Pickup/Dropoff rows until all needed people are transported. Ceremony @ Enter the exact ceremony start time — this acts as a “cutoff” marker. After Ceremony Continue filling out return trips, late arrivals, post-reception runs, etc. Notes on the right side can be used for: “We wait for this shuttle before starting ceremony” “Family-only shuttle” “Pre-Ceremony Photos Shuttle” “Groomsmen Riding Separately” If you’re unsure how many runs you’ll need — just fill in the first few pickups and we’ll work backwards from your ceremony time. Decor Layout — Important Instructions List every single item you want placed on-site — whether decorative or functional. If it’s important and it isn’t listed, we will make our best guess on where it goes.If you don’t want guessing, make sure it’s written in clearly. This also applies to anything you want to borrow from Rixey — if it’s not listed here, it isn’t guaranteed to be set out. If you’re unsure of exact layout yet, list everything under “TBD” and we’ll sort it later. Delete or add tables, or spaces (for instance if you are having a memorial chair at the ceremony), or who is bringing in cookies for the cookie table. This is our check list of things coming in and out. It is also your packing list. Instructions for the Seating Chart Tab Please use the template below to plan your reception seating. Each row = one table. Example: “Table 1/A” goes on one row, “Table 2/B” on the next, etc. First column (“Who is the table?”) — Describe what group this table represents, e.g. Bride’s Family / Groom’s Friends / College Roommates / Work Friends / Kids / VIPs, etc. Under each Table Header — Type the guest names seated at that table. Please also note next to names (or in brackets): High chair needed (e.g. “Emma Smith (High Chair)”) Kids (if not already listed in their own group) Guests with mobility or special considerations (elderly, wheelchair users, hearing/vision needs, etc.) The count at the bottom of each column will automatically total how many guests are seated there. ➡️ If you extend or combine tables, you can add more rows or duplicate the layout as needed. Just make sure the total count reflects the number of seats. If you’re unsure about certain guests, feel free to leave them in a "To Be Placed" section at the bottom, and we’ll decide together. Don’t stress about perfection — start with the definite groupings. We’ll review and finalize this together during the Final Walkthrough. Instructions for the Table Sizes & Linens Tab This tab helps us determine how many tablecloths/linens you’ll need for your reception and rehearsal events. You do not need to have this fully filled in right now.We’ll finalize it together at the Final Walkthrough, once your seating chart and decor layout are confirmed. If you want to start early, here’s how to use it: Each line represents one table size or style (ex: 5’ Round, 6’ Banquet, Cocktail Table, Gift Table, etc.) Enter how many of each you expect to use based on your seating plan or decor setup. If you're unsure, just estimate or leave blank — even “Unsure — likely 15 rounds + 4 rectangles” is helpful. Linen Quantity Guideline (for reference) Most weddings at Rixey use: Wedding Size Estimated Linen Count Small (under 80 guests) ~12–20 linens Medium (80–130 guests) ~20–25 linens Large (130+ guests) ~25–30 linens This includes: Guest Tables Head Table or Sweetheart Table Buffet / Dessert / Favor / Drinks Tables Gift / Guestbook / Memorial Table Cocktail Tables (if using) Bottom line: If you don’t know yet, leave it. We’ll calculate the exact count for you once your seating chart and decor layout are done. Instructions for the Hair & Makeup Schedule Tab Please use this tab to list everyone who will be getting hair and/or makeup done on the wedding day. Each row = one person. Once complete, send this list to your hair/makeup artist so they can build a full schedule. How the Process Works We will set a “Ready By” time based on your ceremony time, first look, or photo schedule. You give this tab (or a screenshot of it) to your stylist. Your stylist creates the official getting-ready timeline. You can either email it back to me, or paste it directly into this tab beneath your list. If You’re Unsure… If you don’t know exact services yet — just list names and we’ll refine later. If someone might get hair or makeup — add them with “TBD.” If someone needs to be done first, make a quick note next to their name. If this tab is not filled in, I will assume: Role Default Service Assumed Bride Hair + Makeup Bridesmaids Hair + Makeup Mothers Makeup Only Flower Girls Hair Only (if applicable) Don’t worry about formatting — just start listing people. We’ll review and finalize during the Final Walkthrough. Instructions for the Ceremony / Rehearsal Information Tab Please use this tab to outline your ceremony order, roles, and seating. Processional (Columns A–B): List who is walking down the aisle, and with whom. Example: “Mother of Groom (name)— escorted by Stepfather of Groom (name)” Include ages for children (flower girls, ring bearers) and note if anyone has mobility concerns (e.g., cane, wheelchair, slower pace). Recessional (Columns C–D): Record how everyone will exit after the ceremony. Typically this mirrors the processional but in reverse order, starting with the couple. You can just write "in reverse" Front Row Seating (Columns F–G): Use this section to assign seats for immediate family. We recommend no more than 8 total per side (to keep it simple and avoid politics immediate family only and the number of people on each side doesn't have to match). Side 1 = Bride’s side, Side 2 = Groom’s side (from the center aisle out). Example Section (below): Shows a sample order for reference. You can use it as a guide but adapt it to your unique family and wedding party. ➡️ Important Notes: Please flag anyone with special needs, elderly guests, or children so we can plan accordingly. Don’t worry about perfection — start with your best draft. We’ll finalize together during rehearsal. Instructions for the Bedroom Assignment Tab Use this tab to assign overnight accommodations for anyone staying on-site at Rixey Manor or the Cottage. Each row represents one bedroom, already labeled with: Room Name (e.g. Newlywed Suite, Maple Bedroom, Cottage, etc.) Details about the bed setup or accessibility notes (e.g. “Queen bed + bathtub”, “Shared shower”, “Best for early risers / quiet sleepers”) Please fill in: Friday Night — who is sleeping in each room Saturday Night — same for the wedding night, if different A few helpful guidelines: Pets are usually happier (and less stressed) staying in the Cottage, away from the reception noise. If someone snores loudly, wakes up very early, or needs a quiet/private space, please assign rooms accordingly. Kids or guests with mobility or medical needs should be placed closest to bathrooms or ground floor rooms. If people are only staying one night, feel free to note that next to their name. Instructions for the Drinks List Tab Use this page to plan out what alcohol and bar supplies you’ll be bringing. The recommendations at the top are based on a 100-person wedding, but feel free to adjust up or down depending on your guest count and drinking style. What You Need To Fill In: Beer / Wine / Liquor / Mixers / Water Bottles Under each section, list: Type (e.g. Pacifico, Chardonnay, Vodka, Coke, etc.) Quantity (kegs, cases, bottles, etc.) You don’t have to follow our brand suggestions — those are just examples! Specialty Cocktails (optional) If you're doing a Signature Drink, write: Cocktail Name (e.g. “Spicy Marg”, “Elderflower Paloma”) Recipe or Ingredients Needed If you don’t know exact measurements, just list the ingredients and your bartender can finalize it. Water, Soda & Ice We’ve included reminders at the bottom — roughly 1 pound of ice per guest is ideal. If unsure, just use the notes section to describe what you think you’ll do.

title: Google Planning Sheet
description: Details and guidelines for filling in your planning sheet.
content: Google Planning Sheet We provide a collaborative Google Sheets workbook customized for your wedding. This planning sheet has multiple tabs (pages) covering everything from your timeline, vendor contacts, and layout plans to décor checklists and music selections. It’s a living document that both you and our team can update, ensuring everyone stays on the same page. A Few Important Notes Before You Begin: If you cannot open or access this sheet — email me immediately so I can resend it or adjust permissions. Don’t wait. Only fill in the tabs that apply to your wedding. If a tab doesn’t apply (e.g. no rehearsal dinner, no shuttle, no bedroom assignments), feel free to delete it or simply leave it blank. You may write comments anywhere — questions, “not sure yet,” or options you’re debating. I’d rather see a half-filled plan than wait for perfection. If you leave notes like “Need Instructions” or “Not sure what to do here,” I’ll guide you during our next check-in. If you’d like instructions on how to edit in Google Sheets , how to highlight cells , or how to add comments , I’m happy to add a Technology Help tab — just say the word. Top Sheet — Start Here (But Don’t Stress About Completing It Yet) This is your quick-glance overview of the weekend — the sheet we refer back to constantly when coordinating with vendors, checking timing, or solving last-minute mysteries (“What time is the rehearsal again?”). You do not need to fill in every box right away. Just start with what you know. We will go back through every field together during your Final Walkthrough. Here’s how to approach it: Names & Parents’ Names — Fill in yours and your parents’ full names. This helps with welcome speeches, seating, and staff knowing who’s who. “Have We Met Them?” — Just write Yes / No / Met Once / Not Yet — this lets us know whether intros are needed. Rehearsal Time and Rehearsal Guest Count — Fill it in if you’ve already decided. If not sure yet, leave it. Rehearsal Dinner Onsite or Offsite? — Just write Onsite or Offsite. Details come later. Wedding Colors — Loose description is fine (e.g. Dusty Blue + Sage, or Autumn Florals). Helps staff match linens and decor placement. Wedding Date / Guest Count / Ceremony Start Time / Sunset Please fill these in early — these drive all scheduling (ceremony lighting, photography, shuttle timing, etc.). Allergies Section Leave blank if there are none yet — otherwise list: Who the allergy is for What it is (e.g. nuts, shellfish, bees) Severity (e.g. mild / EpiPen / anaphylactic) Have you alerted the caterer? — Yes/No Staying overnight? — Only matters for Manor guests Extra Staff Section You’ll see a reminder of Rixey’s staffing policy here — 1 bartender per X guests, and Extra Hands for setup/cleanup if full-service catering isn’t handling it. You don’t need to know final numbers yet. For now, just answer roughly: How many bartenders you expect to need each day (Fri/Sat/Sun) How many Extra Hands you expect — if you’re unsure, write “Not sure yet” and we’ll decide together. Venmo Handle — please fill this in early so we can coordinate payment smoothly. Remember: This tab is meant to start the conversation, not finish it.Fill in what you can — even half answers are fine — and we’ll review every row line by line together at the Final Walkthrough. Questions — Please Answer When You Can This tab is simply a list of questions we’ll need answered before the wedding. If you already know the answer — fill it in.If you don’t — leave it blank and we’ll go over it together at your Final Walkthrough. No pressure to complete everything at once. This is just where we collect decisions so they’re all in one place. Vendors Please fill out one row per vendor. If you don’t have all the details yet, just fill in what you know — blanks are fine and we’ll finish it together at your Final Walkthrough. Column Header What to Enter Vendor The type of service they are providing (e.g. Catering for Wedding, DJ, Florist, Shuttle Service). Keep categories consistent so it’s easy to skim. Name The business name or individual’s name (whichever is most accurate to who is actually showing up). Contact Phone Number Best direct number for day-of coordination. If they have both office and cell, use who will be on-site. Arrival Time When they are scheduled to arrive for setup. If unsure, leave blank and we’ll confirm later. Departure Time When they expect to leave / finish breakdown. Leave blank if not known yet. Have they worked here before? Yes / No / Not Sure — this just helps us know how much direction they’ll need on-site. Have you uploaded contract to HB? Yes / No — this is to confirm whether their contract is already stored in HoneyBook. Instagram Handle (if you know it) Optional — add if you'd like us to tag vendors in social media posts afterward. Notes: Any special instructions, delivery timing requests, or reminders (e.g. “Deliver Thursday,” “Needs access to power,” “Bringing their own ice”). Friday & Sunday Timeline — Fill As You Know It, We’ll Finalize Together This tab helps organize who’s arriving when and what’s happening on Friday (check-in / rehearsal) and Sunday (checkout / brunch or cleanup).You do not need to fill in everything at once — just start entering times and vendors as plans come together. We will confirm all timing and logistics at your Final Walkthrough. Friday Section (Check-In / Rehearsal Day) There are two parts to fill: Vendors Involved (Top Table) List only the vendors who are delivering or setting up on Friday. Fill in Vendor Type / Company Name / Contact / Arrival / Departure if known. If you’re unsure of timing yet — leave it blank. Timeline (Time Block Table) Use the Time Block / Time / Event / Those Involved / Notes fields to map out the flow. Start with what you know (e.g. “4:00 PM Check-In”, “5:30 PM Rehearsal”). Leave anything blank that is still TBD — we will line it up with you later. At the bottom is a Rehearsal Dinner Question Section — simply answer each question in the blue cells next to it. Short answers are fine. Sunday Section (Checkout / Brunch / Cleanup) Same format as Friday — Vendor Table on top, Timeline Table below. Only fill this section if you’re staying for Sunday brunch or hosting guests onsite. If you’re not doing a brunch or extended stay — just write “Not Staying Sunday” at the top of the Sunday section. You’ll also see an “Average Time Estimates” reminder (e.g. Cleaning Up = 2 hours). These are just guidelines — feel free to adjust based on your plans. Reminder: Start light — even half-filled is perfect.Anything unconfirmed will be locked in during your Final Walkthrough Saturday Timeline — Start with Just the Two Key Details This tab doesn’t need to be perfect — it just gives us your starting outline for how you picture the day flowing. Before anything else, please answer: What time does your ceremony begin? Will you be doing a First Look? (Yes / No / Not Sure Yet is fine.) Once those are filled in: If you already have a general order in mind (e.g. Photos → Ceremony → Cocktail Hour → Dinner → Dancing), start adding things into the Time / Event / Those Involved columns. If not — leave it blank. We’ll build the full schedule with you using the sample timelines in the dedicated Timeline Example section later in the sheet. You can also write notes like “Want sunset photos before dinner” or “Prefer longer cocktail hour” — even loose preferences are helpful. No need for exact minutes — just the anchors.Ceremony time + First Look answer are the only must-haves right now. Shuttle Schedule — Fill This Once You Know Ceremony Time Use this tab to lay out all shuttle runs to and from the hotel/venue. You’ll see two sets of tables: A blank version at the top (for you to fill out) A sample version underneath (for reference only — do not edit that one) How to fill the blank table: Column What to Enter Shuttle 1 / Shuttle 2 Each column is one vehicle. If you only have one shuttle, just use the first column. Pick up at Hotel Enter the pickup time from the hotel/lodging. Drop off at Manor Enter the expected arrival time at Rixey. …then alternate Pickup/Dropoff rows until all needed people are transported. Ceremony @ Enter the exact ceremony start time — this acts as a “cutoff” marker. After Ceremony Continue filling out return trips, late arrivals, post-reception runs, etc. Notes on the right side can be used for: “We wait for this shuttle before starting ceremony” “Family-only shuttle” “Pre-Ceremony Photos Shuttle” “Groomsmen Riding Separately” If you’re unsure how many runs you’ll need — just fill in the first few pickups and we’ll work backwards from your ceremony time. Decor Layout — Important Instructions List every single item you want placed on-site — whether decorative or functional. If it’s important and it isn’t listed, we will make our best guess on where it goes.If you don’t want guessing, make sure it’s written in clearly. This also applies to anything you want to borrow from Rixey — if it’s not listed here, it isn’t guaranteed to be set out. If you’re unsure of exact layout yet, list everything under “TBD” and we’ll sort it later. Delete or add tables, or spaces (for instance if you are having a memorial chair at the ceremony), or who is bringing in cookies for the cookie table. This is our check list of things coming in and out. It is also your packing list. Instructions for the Seating Chart Tab Please use the template below to plan your reception seating. Each row = one table. Example: “Table 1/A” goes on one row, “Table 2/B” on the next, etc. First column (“Who is the table?”) — Describe what group this table represents, e.g. Bride’s Family / Groom’s Friends / College Roommates / Work Friends / Kids / VIPs, etc. Under each Table Header — Type the guest names seated at that table. Please also note next to names (or in brackets): High chair needed (e.g. “Emma Smith (High Chair)”) Kids (if not already listed in their own group) Guests with mobility or special considerations (elderly, wheelchair users, hearing/vision needs, etc.) The count at the bottom of each column will automatically total how many guests are seated there. ➡️ If you extend or combine tables, you can add more rows or duplicate the layout as needed. Just make sure the total count reflects the number of seats. If you’re unsure about certain guests, feel free to leave them in a "To Be Placed" section at the bottom, and we’ll decide together. Don’t stress about perfection — start with the definite groupings. We’ll review and finalize this together during the Final Walkthrough. Instructions for the Table Sizes & Linens Tab This tab helps us determine how many tablecloths/linens you’ll need for your reception and rehearsal events. You do not need to have this fully filled in right now.We’ll finalize it together at the Final Walkthrough, once your seating chart and decor layout are confirmed. If you want to start early, here’s how to use it: Each line represents one table size or style (ex: 5’ Round, 6’ Banquet, Cocktail Table, Gift Table, etc.) Enter how many of each you expect to use based on your seating plan or decor setup. If you're unsure, just estimate or leave blank — even “Unsure — likely 15 rounds + 4 rectangles” is helpful. Linen Quantity Guideline (for reference) Most weddings at Rixey use: Wedding Size Estimated Linen Count Small (under 80 guests) ~12–20 linens Medium (80–130 guests) ~20–25 linens Large (130+ guests) ~25–30 linens This includes: Guest Tables Head Table or Sweetheart Table Buffet / Dessert / Favor / Drinks Tables Gift / Guestbook / Memorial Table Cocktail Tables (if using) Bottom line: If you don’t know yet, leave it. We’ll calculate the exact count for you once your seating chart and decor layout are done. Instructions for the Hair & Makeup Schedule Tab Please use this tab to list everyone who will be getting hair and/or makeup done on the wedding day. Each row = one person. Once complete, send this list to your hair/makeup artist so they can build a full schedule. How the Process Works We will set a “Ready By” time based on your ceremony time, first look, or photo schedule. You give this tab (or a screenshot of it) to your stylist. Your stylist creates the official getting-ready timeline. You can either email it back to me, or paste it directly into this tab beneath your list. If You’re Unsure… If you don’t know exact services yet — just list names and we’ll refine later. If someone might get hair or makeup — add them with “TBD.” If someone needs to be done first, make a quick note next to their name. If this tab is not filled in, I will assume: Role Default Service Assumed Bride Hair + Makeup Bridesmaids Hair + Makeup Mothers Makeup Only Flower Girls Hair Only (if applicable) Don’t worry about formatting — just start listing people. Each row = one table. Example: “Table 1/A” goes on one row, “Table 2/B” on the next, etc. First column (“Who is the table?”) — Describe what group this table represents, e.g. Bride’s Family / Groom’s Friends / College Roommates / Work Friends / Kids / VIPs, etc. Under each Table Header — Type the guest names seated at that table. Please also note next to names (or in brackets): High chair needed (e.g. “Emma Smith (High Chair)”) Kids (if not already listed in their own group) Guests with mobility or special considerations (elderly, wheelchair users, hearing/vision needs, etc.) The count at the bottom of each column will automatically total how many guests are seated there. ➡️ If you extend or combine tables, you can add more rows or duplicate the layout as needed. Just make sure the total count reflects the number of seats. If you’re unsure about certain guests, feel free to leave them in a "To Be Placed" section at the bottom, and we’ll decide together. Don’t stress about perfection — start with the definite groupings. We’ll review and finalize this together during the Final Walkthrough. Instructions for the Table Sizes & Linens Tab This tab helps us determine how many tablecloths/linens you’ll need for your reception and rehearsal events. You do not need to have this fully filled in right now.We’ll finalize it together at the Final Walkthrough, once your seating chart and decor layout are confirmed. If you want to start early, here’s how to use it: Each line represents one table size or style (ex: 5’ Round, 6’ Banquet, Cocktail Table, Gift Table, etc.) Enter how many of each you expect to use based on your seating plan or decor setup. If you're unsure, just estimate or leave blank — even “Unsure — likely 15 rounds + 4 rectangles” is helpful. Linen Quantity Guideline (for reference) Most weddings at Rixey use: Wedding Size Estimated Linen Count Small (under 80 guests) ~12–20 linens Medium (80–130 guests) ~20–25 linens Large (130+ guests) ~25–30 linens This includes: Guest Tables Head Table or Sweetheart Table Buffet / Dessert / Favor / Drinks Tables Gift / Guestbook / Memorial Table Cocktail Tables (if using) Bottom line: If you don’t know yet, leave it. We’ll calculate the exact count for you once your seating chart and decor layout are done. Instructions for the Hair & Makeup Schedule Tab Please use this tab to list everyone who will be getting hair and/or makeup done on the wedding day. Each row = one person. Once complete, send this list to your hair/makeup artist so they can build a full schedule. How the Process Works We will set a “Ready By” time based on your ceremony time, first look, or photo schedule. You give this tab (or a screenshot of it) to your stylist. Your stylist creates the official getting-ready timeline. You can either email it back to me, or paste it directly into this tab beneath your list. If You’re Unsure… If you don’t know exact services yet — just list names and we’ll refine later. If someone might get hair or makeup — add them with “TBD.” If someone needs to be done first, make a quick note next to their name. If this tab is not filled in, I will assume: Role Default Service Assumed Bride Hair + Makeup Bridesmaids Hair + Makeup Mothers Makeup Only Flower Girls Hair Only (if applicable) Don’t worry about formatting — just start listing people. We’ll review and finalize during the Final Walkthrough. Instructions for the Ceremony / Rehearsal Information Tab Please use this tab to outline your ceremony order, roles, and seating. Processional (Columns A–B): List who is walking down the aisle, and with whom. Example: “Mother of Groom (name)— escorted by Stepfather of Groom (name)” Include ages for children (flower girls, ring bearers) and note if anyone has mobility concerns (e.g., cane, wheelchair, slower pace). Recessional (Columns C–D): Record how everyone will exit after the ceremony. Typically this mirrors the processional but in reverse order, starting with the couple. You can just write "in reverse" Front Row Seating (Columns F–G): Use this section to assign seats for immediate family. We recommend no more than 8 total per side (to keep it simple and avoid politics immediate family only and the number of people on each side doesn't have to match). Side 1 = Bride’s side, Side 2 = Groom’s side (from the center aisle out). Example Section (below): Shows a sample order for reference. You can use it as a guide but adapt it to your unique family and wedding party. ➡️ Important Notes: Please flag anyone with special needs, elderly guests, or children so we can plan accordingly. Don’t worry about perfection — start with your best draft. We’ll finalize together during rehearsal. Instructions for the Bedroom Assignment Tab Use this tab to assign overnight accommodations for anyone staying on-site at Rixey Manor or the Cottage. Each row represents one bedroom, already labeled with: Room Name (e.g. Newlywed Suite, Maple Bedroom, Cottage, etc.) Details about the bed setup or accessibility notes (e.g. “Queen bed + bathtub”, “Shared shower”, “Best for early risers / quiet sleepers”) Please fill in: Friday Night — who is sleeping in each room Saturday Night — same for the wedding night, if different A few helpful guidelines: Pets are usually happier (and less stressed) staying in the Cottage, away from the reception noise. If someone snores loudly, wakes up very early, or needs a quiet/private space, please assign rooms accordingly. Kids or guests with mobility or medical needs should be placed closest to bathrooms or ground floor rooms. If people are only staying one night, feel free to note that next to their name. Instructions for the Drinks List Tab Use this page to plan out what alcohol and bar supplies you’ll be bringing. The recommendations at the top are based on a 100-person wedding, but feel free to adjust up or down depending on your guest count and drinking style. What You Need To Fill In: Beer / Wine / Liquor / Mixers / Water Bottles Under each section, list: Type (e.g. Pacifico, Chardonnay, Vodka, Coke, etc.) Quantity (kegs, cases, bottles, etc.) You don’t have to follow our brand suggestions — those are just examples! Specialty Cocktails (optional) If you're doing a Signature Drink, write: Cocktail Name (e.g. “Spicy Marg”, “Elderflower Paloma”) Recipe or Ingredients Needed If you don’t know exact measurements, just list the ingredients and your bartender can finalize it. Water, Soda & Ice We’ve included reminders at the bottom — roughly 1 pound of ice per guest is ideal. If unsure, just use the notes section to describe what you think you’ll do.

title: Rentals Guidance
description: Tips on linens, arbors, sparklers, and signage rentals.
content: Rentals Guidance Highlights Rixey provides tables, white chairs, basic ceremony arches, heaters, extension cords, and some décor. Linens are almost always rented— DO NOT BUY (ask us why)! Specialty chairs, china, glassware, tents, lighting, and lounge furniture are optional rentals. Rental timing matters: aim for delivery Thursday/Friday, pick-up Monday. Always clarify who’s handling post-event dish/glassware cleanup (caterer vs venue). Beware of cheap/low-quality rental sources—use reputable companies. We’ll review your rental quotes with you to avoid unnecessary costs. What Rixey Provides (Included in Venue Rental) Tables & Chairs: Standard tables + white chairs. We have 200 chairs total. Ceremony Items: Easel, multiple arches/arbors (white birch, round metal, hexagon wood). Décor Pieces: See Décor Borrowing List for extras you can use. Practical Items: Extension cords, power strips, etc. Linens Almost always rented or ordered via caterer. Buying is strongly discouraged: linens arrive wrinkled and require pressing. Rented linens: arrive pressed/hung, returned dirty (easy!). Typical costs: ~$19 for basic polyester, $30+ for specialty fabrics. Pro tip: Arrange Monday pick-up if possible (lets us reuse for Sunday brunch). Color tip: dark cloths + lighter napkins = hides spills, looks elegant. Chairs Provided: white chairs (classic, clean). Upgrade rentals: Cross-backs: $16 apox Cost-saving idea: rent special chairs just for sweetheart table and use standard for guests. Delivery & storage: we have space if chairs arrive early. Dinnerware & Glassware If using full-service caterer , they often supply plates, silverware, and glasses. If DIY/food trucks: choose between disposables (many upscale options) or rentals. Rental costs add up: multiple glasses per guest (water, wine, champagne). Hybrid approach saves $$: Rent real linens + silverware. Use disposables for wine/beer or dessert. Clarify cleanup: rental companies expect dishes/glasses rinsed + reboxed. Ask if your caterer handles this. Tents Needed if guest count >100 or if you want outdoor reception. Indoor capacity: ~100 seated in ballroom/dining. Tent sizes: e.g., 40’ x 70’ can fit ~200 with dance floor. Costs: several thousand dollars. Book early. Extras often required: lighting, flooring, dance floor. Install: usually Thursday/Friday before. Trusted local vendors: Sammy’s Rentals Specialty Décor & Furniture Lounge seating, vintage furniture, themed bars, etc. are rental options. Popular ideas: Lounge set for older guests away from loud music. Unique bar furniture. Note: The Manor’s inherent beauty means you may not need extras. Lighting Rixey’s existing lighting: ballroom chandeliers + outdoor string lights to light food trucks. Rental/upgrade options: Market lights: Tents/patios. Uplighting: Ballroom or outdoors (often via DJ). DJ lights: Highly recommended—adds energy to dance floor. Candles: allowed if in holders. We provide glass votives. You provide tea lights (real or LED). No oil lamps allowed. Dance Floor Ballroom hardwood floor = dance floor (no rental needed). If tenting outdoors: rent portable dance floor but not necessary. Delivery & Pickup Coordination Schedule deliveries with us in advance. Typical schedule: delivery Thursday/Friday, pick-up Monday (ideal). If vendor requires same-night pickup (rare but possible for lights/sound), let us know—we’ll accommodate. Things We Don’t Recommend Renting/Buying Buying linens: Too much ironing—rent instead. Too many signs: Focus on key ones (welcome, bar, seating chart). Guests don’t need labels for everything. Cheap large items: Low-quality tents/chairs look shabby—use trusted vendors. Silk florals outdoors: Prohibited (don’t biodegrade). Real petals only. Leveraging Vendors Florists: May provide vases, candleholders, arches. Caterers: Often supply cake stands, beverage dispensers, linens. Always check with them before renting extras. Use our Décor Borrowing List too—saves money and effort. Conclusion Rentals should fill gaps—not overwhelm your budget. Invest in impact items like quality linens or a tent (if needed), and lean on what Rixey + your vendors already provide. Package deals and negotiations can save a lot; we’ll happily review quotes with you to cut unnecessary items.

title: Back-to-Back Wedding Logistics
description: What happens when there's another wedding the day after yours.
content:
Back-to-Back Wedding Logistics

If there's a wedding scheduled the day after yours (e.g., you're Saturday, they're Sunday):

Your wedding day: The other couple will NOT drop off items or visit Rixey on your wedding day. Your day is completely yours. If they need to drop off items early, everything is stored entirely separately — typically in the drinks room or cottage — so nothing gets mixed up.

Saturday night: Everything must be cleaned up and packed away by 11pm when the contract ends. We handle the actual cleaning, but your personal items need to be gathered and ready to go.

Sunday pickup: We can store your items in the drinks room or cottage for Sunday or Monday pickup. There's usually a dedicated pickup window by 11am Sunday. Depending on the schedule, timing can be flexible — for example, you can swing by the cottage anytime after brunch.

Your stuff stays your stuff — we're very used to keeping things separate between back-to-back events.

title: Clean-Up Guidance
description: How to manage cleanup and checkout smoothly.
content: Clean-Up Guidance Highlights Rixey handles the heavy cleaning — you only need to pack your own belongings and décor. If you’re here for the weekend, leave most clean-up for Sunday morning . Night-of: we secure cards, gifts, and leftover alcohol for safekeeping. Sunday: strip beds, gather towels, pack personal items first , then décor. Plan ahead for who takes alcohol, gifts, décor, and florals home. Do a final walkthrough of all rooms before departing. Our team does the mopping, trash removal, and deep clean after you leave. Clean-Up Overview After the last dance and goodbyes, comes the not-so-glamorous part: wrapping up and cleaning up. Don’t worry — you won’t be scrubbing floors or mopping (we handle that). You’ll just need to gather your décor and personal items. Our goal is to make this as painless as possible, especially if you’re a little tired (or hungover!) on Sunday morning. Night-Of vs. Next-Day If you’re staying the weekend, leave most of the work for Sunday morning. Check-out is 10am (unless you’ve arranged a Brunch Upgrade). Start cleanup around 8:30–9am so you have 1–2 hours. Enlist a few early risers to help box and load. Personal Belongings First Begin upstairs in the bedrooms. Strip beds (sheets + pillowcases) and gather used towels → place in the laundry room behind the kitchen. Do a final sweep of drawers, closets, and bathrooms (chargers in outlets, toiletries in showers often get left). Load personal suitcases into cars first so décor doesn’t get buried. Décor and Leftovers Staff may consolidate décor into one room overnight. On Sunday, re-pack into your labeled bins/boxes. We gather unused items (extra favors, ribbons, etc.) and pack them too. Trash: we’ll collect, but please bag obvious trash and leave it in the utility room. Alcohol: Keep the original boxes for easy transport. Split leftovers between family cars if needed — couples rarely have space for it all. Unopened cases may be returnable (stores often allow it). Food: Caterer usually leave leftovers. Take home leftover cake. Bring Coolers if you have a long drive Any leftovers can be donated/left with us. Florals: Send home with guests or take them yourself. We’ll donate or use left over florals. Florist usually reclaims rental vases; if you borrowed ours, just leave them. Rentals: External rental items (linens, furniture, tents) are usually picked up Monday. Don’t pack rental or venue items into your bins by accident (cake stands, linens, etc.). Loading Vehicles Assign a “ car Tetris master ” to load efficiently. Keep delicate items (dress, bouquet, paperwork) separate from heavy décor. Consider who’s taking what: alcohol, décor, gifts, etc. Couples often only leave with personal bags while family SUVs take the rest. We will have gathered irreplaceable items into a "precious" box - usually includes sentimental or valuable decor items, guest book etc. Things that cannot be replaced. Final Walkthrough Before departure: Check all bedrooms, bathrooms, bar, kitchen, and outdoor areas . Don’t forget signed marriage license , phones, chargers, jewelry, or signs placed outside. Anything left behind goes to our lost-and-found (we’ll contact you). Our Cleaning Crew Once you depart, our team does the deep clean — mopping, linen laundering, and breakdown. We just ask you to: Bag trash. Don’t leave furniture in chaos (we or rental company will reset big items). Let us know of any spills/breakages (we likely already handled it, but communication helps). Timing With brunch extension: checkout is 1pm Sunday . Please be mindful of timing — we may have another event to reset for, and we almost always have meetings scheduled to start after check out. The Sentimental Side Cleanup can feel emotional — your wedding is officially over. Take a moment in the empty ballroom, snap a “morning after” photo, or do one last dance. Many couples love that quiet memory.

Timing With brunch extension: checkout is 1pm Sunday . Please be mindful of timing — we may have another event to reset for, and we almost always have meetings scheduled to start after check out. The Sentimental Side Cleanup can feel emotional — your wedding is officially over. Take a moment in the empty ballroom, snap a “morning after” photo, or do one last dance. Many couples love that quiet memory.

title: Vendor Confirmations
description: Checklist for confirming arrival times, contracts, and final details.
content: Vendor Confirmations Highlights Confirm final headcounts, timelines, and arrival/departure plans with every vendor 1–3 weeks out. Clarify vendor meals and when/where they’ll eat. Close out final balances, paperwork, insurance, and permits. Share day-of emergency contacts (Rixey coordinator/lead) so vendors call us, not you. Reconfirm rehearsal time with the wedding party and officiant. As you approach the final stretch (about 1–3 weeks out), it’s crucial to confirm all the details with your vendors. Think of this as a friendly “check-in” to make sure everyone is on the same page. Here’s a quick guide to vendor confirmations: UPLOAD ALL MOST RECENT CONTRACTS FROM ANY ONE YOU HAVE GIVEN ANY MONEY TO. Headcounts & Timelines Reach out to each vendor with your final guest count and confirm the timing we’ve set. For example, confirm with the caterer the number of guests (including any vendor meals needed) and the dinner serve time. Confirm with the DJ/band the start/end times and when they should arrive for setup. Confirm with the photographer the start time (when they should be at Rixey) and if you’ve decided on any first-look timing or specific photo moments. Essentially, any timing details in your final timeline—communicate those. It may seem redundant if it’s in the contract, but it never hurts to double-confirm. Confirm with your florist that your personal items will be there when the photographer arrives. Arrival and Departure Logistics Email each vendor to verify what time they plan to arrive at Rixey Manor on the event day (or day before, if applicable) and approximately how long they’ll need for setup/breakdown. For instance, “Hi florist, just confirming you’ll arrive at 1pm on Saturday to set up centerpieces, and you expect to finish by 3pm. Let me know if you need anything upon arrival.” If any vendor needs extra time or wants to come earlier, coordinate that with us. We generally have the venue open for vendor access by 9–10am on the wedding day (earlier if needed for something major like a tent setup). Vendor Meals If you’re providing meals for vendors (which is customary for those staying through the reception like photographers, videographers, DJ/band, planner), let those vendors know the plan. Often the caterer will count vendor meals in your final count, but confirm with vendors what time they’ll eat. We suggest scheduling vendor meals as soon as dinner service begins for guests. Communicate to the photographer/DJ that we’ll have them eat when you do, so they don’t miss important moments. And ensure your caterer knows how many vendor meals to prepare. Typically vendors appreciate being fed—a hungry DJ is not a happy DJ! Final Balances and Paperwork Confirm with each vendor that you’re squared away on any final payments or required paperwork. If a vendor requires a final guest count (caterer, rental company for chairs/tables, etc.), make sure they got it by their deadline. If a vendor hasn’t received the final payment or needs a signed addendum, now’s the time to get that done so nobody is chasing money on the day of. This might also include giving the officiant the marriage license at the rehearsal (don’t forget that license on rehearsal day!). Special Requests or Reminders Use the confirmation touchpoint to remind vendors of any unique requests you have communicated. For example: Photographer: “We’ll want a photo with our college friends at the reception—please remind us if we forget!” and confirm the specific family formal shot list you gave them. DJ: “Remember we want to keep songs to radio-edits (no super long versions) and we’ll do last dance at 9:45.” Also send them the final order of reception entrances if you haven’t yet. Florist: “Don’t forget, we’ll reuse the ceremony arch flowers on our sweetheart table as discussed.” (A great idea to repurpose blooms). Bakery: “We’ll be doing cake cutting right after dinner, approximately 7:15pm.” (They may need to know when to deliver the cake by and if you’re saving the top tier). Transportation: Confirm the shuttle schedule (times/locations) and make sure the company has an accurate count of how many people to expect on each run. Any deliveries: Confirm addresses and drop-off times (Rixey’s address is 9155 Pleasant Hill Road, Rixeyville, Culpeper VA … we’ll make sure vendors have directions). Emergency Contacts Provide each vendor with a day-of contact (often our business number) other than you for any issues. Usually we’ll handle this: we often give out our number or the lead coordinator’s number to vendors. You can let them know: “If you need anything on the day, contact Rixey staff at 540-212-4545 number or find Isadora/Grace.” That way, if the DJ is stuck in traffic, they alert us and we can adjust timeline, while keeping you calm and blissfully unaware until solved. Double-Check Insurance/Permits By this stage, if any vendor needed to send a Certificate. If uncertain, ask us. The officiant’s credentials we mentioned—confirm they’re authorized. If using a friend officiant, hopefully they’ve sorted their one-time authorization with the circuit court; if not, now is last call to get a backup authorized officiant if needed. Confirm Rehearsal Time While not exactly a vendor, make sure your wedding party/officiant knows when to be at Rixey for the rehearsal (usually the day before, often late afternoon). We’ll confirm that time with you as well, of course, but a group text or email from you to all participants with the final schedule is helpful. By confirming all vendors, you are essentially doing a mini “roll call” and aligning everyone’s watches. It greatly reduces day-of stress because you know everyone has the right info. Many vendors will actually reach out to you a couple weeks out if you haven’t reached them first—they’re pros and want to confirm too. So don’t be shy about responding with all the details. We also strongly encourage you to share our contact info with vendors and vice versa. On the wedding day, let us handle vendor coordination so you can enjoy. If a vendor is late or lost, we will be the ones to get on the phone and direct them. If something’s missing, we’ll problem-solve. Your job will be to sip champagne and get married 😄. So take an afternoon, fire off those confirmation emails, and then you can check that off your to-do list (see To-Do List item “Confirm numbers and arrival times with all vendors”—that’s this!). You’ll feel so much better knowing everyone is set. Related Sections Final Walkthrough Timeline Shuttle Planning Finance & Paperwork Downloads (Optional) Vendor Confirmation Email Templates (Optional) Vendor Meal Count & Timing Sheet

title: Drop-Off Instructions
description: Guidance on where/when to drop alcohol, rentals, and supplies.
content: Drop-Off Instructions Highlights You can drop off items at Rixey Manor up to a week before your wedding. Schedule your drop-off time in advance so we can be ready for you. Ideal items to drop early: alcohol, mixers, disposables, décor, signage, and favors. Alcohol and bar supplies are stored in the Bar Room; décor and other items in the Dining Room. Perishables should be delivered closer to the wedding (usually Friday). We’ll help inventory your drop-off to make sure nothing is missed. Keep valuables (rings, license, tip envelopes) with you until the weekend. Please remember the house, and the team will be in "getting ready mode", this means the Manor may look like it isn't clean and tidy and the staff will probably be in sweats! We offer the convenience of dropping off your wedding items in the week leading up to your event. This can be a lifesaver — instead of showing up on your Move-In Day with a hundred boxes, you can spread the load and let us store things safely until setup. Schedule the Drop-Off Contact us to arrange a date and time. We usually accept drop-offs up to a week before your wedding. Tuesday through Thursday are the most common drop-off days for Saturday weddings. Even if you can’t be there in person, a planner, family member, or friend can bring items — just let us know who to expect. What to Drop Off Bring anything non-perishable that you won’t need before the wedding: Alcohol, sodas, mixers (best done in one trip since they’re heavy) Disposables like cups, napkins, and straws Décor, signage, favors, welcome table items Guest book, card box, photo frames (non-fragile) Dress, suits, shoes! If you have welcome bags for hotel delivery, take those directly to the hotel. If they’re for on-site guests, bring them here. Where It Goes Alcohol and bar supplies: Stored in the Bar Room. We unpack and check quantities so nothing is missed. We may start chilling champagne and will let you know if you’re short on anything. Décor and other items: Stored in the Dining Room. This becomes our staging area until setup begins on Friday. Fragile or valuable items (custom art, delicate toppers) can be placed in a more secure or climate-controlled location. Perishables: Most perishable items (flowers, desserts, cakes) should arrive Friday. If you need special storage (freezer or fridge), let us know. Checklist Upon Drop-Off When you arrive, we’ll do a quick inventory together. Bring your packing list if you have one so we can double-check. We’ll also ask clarifying questions (e.g., “These two boxes say ‘centerpieces’ — will someone be arranging them, or are they pre-made?”). If certain items depend on conditions (like “don’t put out programs if it’s windy”), please flag that for us. Final Delivery on Move-In Day Even with an early drop-off, you’ll likely still bring personal and last-minute items on Move-In Day (Friday): clothing, rings, license, vendor tip envelopes, or any extras. On that day, we’ll help you move décor boxes into the rooms where they’ll actually be used (ceremony, reception, cocktail, etc.). Heavy lifting is on us — you just need to point things in the right direction. Keep Valuables With You Our Manor is safe, but we strongly advise you to keep valuables with you until the weekend. Do not drop off your wedding rings, cash, or gift cards. Bring those when you check in or stay overnight. Why It Helps Dropping off early reduces stress enormously. Couples often say unloading by Thursday let them arrive Friday feeling light and relaxed. After you drop off, we begin subtle prep — checking alcohol amounts, staging décor, and making sure everything is in order. We treat your items as if they were our own, so you can rest easy knowing everything is safe and ready. In Summary Schedule your drop-off, bring the bulk of your items early, keep valuables with you, and let us take it from there. By the time you roll in on Friday, the heavy work will already be done — and you’ll be able to focus on the fun.

By the time you roll in on Friday, the heavy work will already be done — and you’ll be able to focus on the fun.

title: Wedding Week Drop-Off Schedule
description: When couples can start bringing items to the venue during their wedding week.
content:
Wedding Week Drop-Off Schedule

You can start dropping off items at Rixey Manor on Monday of your wedding week. This includes alcohol, decorations, personal items, and anything else you want stored ahead of time.

How it works:
- Schedule your drop-off time via Calendly or text (540) 212-4545.
- If no one is around on a particular day, you can still drop off — just let us know you're coming.
- Early drop-off items go in the Dining Room until official setup begins on Friday.
- Pack and label items by wedding "zone" (ceremony, reception, gift table, sweetheart table, etc.) so setup goes smoothly.

Friday is typically the main setup day. Grace and the team will be working on setup, and you and your bridal party can help with personal touches like bud vase arrangements, place cards, and decor placement.

For perishable items (flowers, cake, food): Friday or Saturday delivery recommended. We have fridge space available — just coordinate in advance.

title: Emergency & Backup Items
description: Quick-access list for things like razors, chargers, ribbon, and thank-you cards.
content: Emergency & Backup Items Highlights Build your own compact day-of emergency kit (travel sizes are perfect). Rixey maintains a comprehensive “oh no!” closet of backups—ask us first before a store run. Bring backups for tech and decor (batteries, spare cards, extra place cards). We have weather and power contingencies covered; loop us in on any special needs. Even with the best planning, sometimes things go awry—a stain on a dress, a headache from the excitement, or a speaker that suddenly needs batteries. We strongly suggest assembling a small emergency kit for the day, and know that Rixey Manor also keeps a comprehensive stash of emergency and backup items just in case. Here’s how to be prepared: Bride’s (or Groom’s) Emergency Kit We keep most things on hand. But these are the MUST brings:- Medication Face and hair products (we have them but your wedding day isn't the time to try a new brand) Lens Solution Eye Drops Dress and Shoes for dancing in What Rixey Has On Hand We maintain our own “oh no!” supply closet. So if you don’t have something, ask us before running to a store. We typically have: pain relievers, bandages, deodorant, toothbrushes & toothpaste, mouthwash, floss, tampons/pads, baby wipes, lint rollers, super glue, safety pins, sewing kit, white chalk (great for covering a stain on a white dress in a pinch), earring backs, Q-tips, tweezers, nail clippers, bug spray, sunscreen, hand warmers, umbrellas, phone chargers, extension cords, duct tape, and even spare votive candles, lighters, scissors, and vases for decor needs. We keep steamers and irons on-site (no need to bring those), as well as snack boxes for quick blood sugar boosts. We also have extra linens, towels, and bedding in case of spills or if someone forgets to pack sheets for an air mattress. Our mantra is, “better to have it and not need it, than need it and not have it.” If you think you might need something obscure, ask us—we might surprise you. Backups for Tech & Decor We recommend bringing backup batteries (and chargers) for any electronics you’re using (like battery-operated candles, portable speakers, or your vows if you typed them on a phone—have a printed copy too!). For decor, pack a few spare place cards, a spare favor if they’re personalized (one or two extra in case of last-minute guest additions), and maybe a few extra invitations/programs for keepsakes or if one gets damaged. We often have blank cards and paper for emergency printing or hand-written signage, so we can make something on the fly if needed (like an extra menu sign or a last-minute “reserved” sign). Weather-Related Backup If it’s going to be hot, have water and maybe small handheld fans for your bridal party. If cold, have shawls or wraps for the ladies in sleeveless dresses. We keep blankets and umbrellas handy for guests if a sudden chill or drizzle hits. Medical Needs We are not medical professionals, but we do have a basic first aid kit on site. If you or a key guest have severe allergies or medical needs (e.g., someone needs an epi-pen), make sure they bring their gear and inform us quietly so we can be aware. We’ve had weddings with on-site nurses or doctors among guests which is reassuring, but if not, we can always call emergency services—we have the address and directions ready. That said, minor things like a scraped knee from dancing too hard—we can handle those with our first aid kit. Peace of Mind Items Sometimes just knowing something is available can relieve stress. For example, a backup boutonniere—if one wilts or falls apart, we can usually make one from extra flowers. Or a spare tie if a groomsman spills wine on his. We even have a spare set of simple wedding bands (a plain gold and a plain silver band) that we keep as props in case the real rings are MIA at ceremony time—true story! Fortunately, they’ve never had to be used, but hey, we have them. The key message here is: Don’t panic. If anything goes wrong or if you realize something’s missing, tell us. There’s a very high chance we have a fix ready. Our team has been known to sew a bridesmaid into a ripped dress, run to CVS for allergy meds, and once, print a copy of a father-of-the-bride’s speech at midnight when he lost his notes. We’ve got your back. So review your personal emergency kit, toss it in your car, and rest easy knowing that between your kit and ours, you’re basically invincible on your wedding day. 😉 Whatever happens, we’ll handle it and keep the day on track.

title: Final Walkthrough Checklist
description: Checklist of what to confirm with vendors and Rixey team.
content: Final Walkthrough Highlights Scheduled 4–6 weeks before your wedding. Required meeting to review timeline, vendors, décor, floor plans, and logistics. Typically takes 2–3 hours, walking through each part of your day. Couples, and key vendors (if available) should attend — not the whole wedding party. We’ll use your shared Google Planning Sheet to finalize details. By the end, you’ll leave with a clear game plan and peace of mind.. About 4–6 weeks before your wedding, we’ll have a Final Walkthrough meeting at Rixey Manor. This is the big planning milestone where we review every aspect of your wedding on-site. By this point, most of your vendors and details will be decided, and it’s the perfect time to iron out logistics and make sure nothing is overlooked. Scheduling We require that the Final Walkthrough take place roughly one to one-and-a-half months prior to your wedding date. We’ll reach out to schedule a date and time that works for everyone. Ideally, both of you attend in person. If one partner or a key vendor can’t, your parent may attend in their place. We recommend keeping the group small — only those directly involved in planning should be there. Too many voices can distract from the purpose of the meeting. What to Expect The Final Walkthrough is thorough and usually takes a couple of hours. We will mentally walk through every minute of your day: the ceremony, cocktail hour, and reception spaces, and discussing setup for each. We’ll review décor placements, seating layout, timeline, vendor logistics, lodging, and more. You don’t need every detail figured out before you arrive — we’ll help fill in the gaps together. By the end, you’ll have a clear game plan and we’ll have updated notes for our staff. What We’ll Review/Finalize Google Sheets Workbook: We use a shared planning workbook with multiple tabs. By Final Walkthrough, fill in as much as you can. We’ll review each tab (Timeline, Vendors, Décor, Questions, etc.) and finalize them. Confirm details on the Front Info tab (guest count, ceremony time, etc.). Vendor tab: names of vendors, arrival times, and contact info. Décor/Setup tab: specifics of what goes where, and any special instructions. Questions tab: open items from you or family members. Vendor Contracts: We’ll confirm we have current contracts for all vendors in HoneyBook. If you’ve made changes (extended hours, switched vendors, etc.), please upload the new version and remove outdated ones. This includes contracts for rehearsal dinners, brunches, or additional events. Timeline: We’ll go through your timeline in detail — from when hair and makeup start, to shuttle departures, to the sequence of reception events. If you don’t have a timeline yet, don’t worry — we’ll build one together. Be prepared to decide when speeches happen, who is giving them, and what time you want photos (like golden hour shots). Floor Plan & Seating: We’ll finalize your table layout: rounds, longs, sweetheart table, or special reserved tables. If your seating chart is ready, we’ll place it; if not, we’ll at least lock in the number of tables and their sizes so you can finish later. Décor Details: We’ll go through each space and discuss décor. For example, “Guest book on the front porch, moved inside after cocktail hour.” Pro tip: label your décor bins by area (ceremony, reception, cake table, etc.). It saves time for everyone. Remaining Questions: If your parents or others have questions but can’t attend, gather them ahead of time. Common ones include parking for handicapped guests, what happens if it rains, or when you can rehearse. No question is too small — this is the time to ask. Inspiration Photos: Upload any must-have inspo shots to HoneyBook. We’ll look at them together and discuss how to achieve your vision (or create smart alternatives if something is out of budget). Meet the Team: You may meet Rixey staff who’ll be working your wedding day. We also love when couples share a photo of themselves — we post it in the staff area so everyone recognizes you and can greet you by name. After the Walkthrough You’ll leave this meeting feeling confident and excited. We’ll update your official documents (timeline, floor plan, etc.) and send reminders for anything still outstanding (marriage license, shuttle confirmations, etc.). From this point forward, we’ll check in regularly to keep everything on track — your job is mostly to enjoy the final month and let us handle the logistics. Remember, the Final Walkthrough is the time to raise anything on your mind, no matter how small. Want to practice your ceremony entrance? We can do that. Not sure when to schedule your first dance? We’ll figure it out. It’s a long meeting, but it’s also fun — a chance to imagine your day from start to finish. By the end, you’ll likely hear us say, “We’ve got this.” And we do. ✅
